title: Does the cyclic group C_4355 admit a (4355,2177,1088)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: Lean would currently formalize only the forced quotient-profile slice. It would not seal the publishable packet because the exact existence or nonexistence theorem for C_4355 is still missing.
micro_paper_assessment: The exact 4355 survivor is still a viable micro-paper target in principle, but the current verified packet is only a structural quotient-profile slice and remains too far from the title theorem for one-shot publication mode.
family_affinity: strong cyclic Hadamard survivor with a unique forced 67-quotient shadow and explicit low-prime quotient profiles
generalization_signal: semiprimitive prime-quotient normalization should transfer to other squarefree cyclic Hadamard survivors when a prime divisor of n is semiprimitive modulo a prime factor of v
strongest_honest_claim: Any cyclic (4355,2177,1088)-difference set would have the unique 67-quotient profile (65,32,...,32) and one of five explicit 13-quotient and five explicit 5-quotient profile classes.
paper_title_hint: Forced Prime-Quotient Profiles in the Cyclic Hadamard Case (4355,2177,1088)
candidate_theorem_slice: Any cyclic (4355,2177,1088)-difference set would have the unique 67-quotient profile (65,32,...,32) and one of five explicit 13-quotient and five explicit 5-quotient profile classes.
publication_packet_quality: credible structural slice, not a paper-ready packet
next_action: Preserve the quotient-profile slice and move the slug aside unless a same-lane compatibility contradiction or direct construction for C_4355 is ready; do not expand into a broader theorem program.
